channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Labrinth Addresses Why His Music Won’t Be in ‘Euphoria’ Season 3: ‘I Don’t Let People Treat Me Like S—t’
item.description:
[Intro music fades out]
Host: Welcome back to our podcast, where we're always on top of the latest music news. Today, we're talking about the HBO hit series Euphoria and its upcoming season 3. You might have noticed that Labrinth, the British singer and producer, won't be involved in the show's music this time around. So, what's behind this decision?
[Short music transition]
Host: According to Labrinth's Instagram Stories, he decided to remove his music from the show due to some behind-the-scenes issues. In a statement, he wrote, "People will comfortably lie in this industry and still call themselves honest people." He also mentioned that when he works for someone, their vision is paramount, but he won't tolerate being treated poorly.
[Short music transition]
Host: This isn't the first time Labrinth has spoken out about his frustrations with the industry. Just a few weeks ago, he criticized his record label and Euphoria in a now-deleted post. Euphoria's creator, Sam Levinson, has since commented on the situation, calling Labrinth an "incredible collaborator" who helped build the show's sound.
[Short music transition]
Host: So, what does this mean for Euphoria's music in season 3? Oscar winner Hans Zimmer will be taking over as the sole composer for the show's eight episodes. Levinson explained that Zimmer is the right choice because each character's storyline is like its own film, and Zimmer's score will bring a new level of depth to the show.
[Short music transition]
Host: And don't worry, Labrinth fans – you can still catch him performing live. He's set to play the outdoor stage at the 2026 Coachella festival. We'll be keeping an eye on his upcoming projects and, of course, the new season of Euphoria.
[Outro music starts playing]
Host: Thanks for tuning in to this episode of our podcast. If you want to stay up-to-date on the latest music news, be sure to follow us on social media and subscribe to our podcast. See you in the next episode!
